Indian Wells: Medvedev Ends Alcaraz’s 16-Game Win Streak

Mar 15, 2026 • 8JJ Sports

Indian Wells: Medvedev Ends Alcaraz’s 16-Game Win Streak

The Indian Wells tournament delivered a major headline as Daniil Medvedev ended Carlos Alcaraz’s remarkable 16-match winning streak in a highly anticipated showdown.

Alcaraz entered the match in outstanding form, having dominated recent tournaments and built one of the strongest winning runs on the ATP Tour this season. However, Medvedev produced a composed and tactical performance to stop the streak.

The match featured powerful rallies, strategic baseline exchanges, and momentum shifts that kept fans on the edge of their seats. Medvedev’s ability to stay consistent under pressure proved decisive.

With this victory, Medvedev not only advanced further in the tournament but also reminded the tennis world of his ability to challenge the sport’s top stars on the biggest stages.

Key Points

  • Daniil Medvedev ended Carlos Alcaraz’s 16-game winning streak
  • The match was one of the most anticipated clashes at Indian Wells
  • Tactical play and consistency helped Medvedev secure the win
  • Alcaraz entered the match in dominant form on the ATP Tour
  • The result adds another dramatic chapter to the Indian Wells tournament
